meta.dc:title: 119 S 284 ENR: To reauthorize the Congressional Award Act.
meta.dc:creator: United States Senate
meta.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

preface.congress: One Hundred Nineteenth Congress of the United States of America
preface.session: A T T H E F I R S T S E S S I O N
preface.enrolledDateline: Begun and held at the City of Washington on Friday, the third day of January, two thousand and twenty five

longTitle.officialTitle: To reauthorize the Congressional Award Act.

enactingFormula.i: Be it enacted by the Senate and House of Representa­tives of the United States of America in Congress assembled,

section.heading: SHORT TITLE.
section.content: This Act may be cited as the “Congressional Award Program Reauthorization Act”.

subsection.heading: In General.—
subsection.content: Section 108 of the Congressional Award Act (2 U.S.C. 808) is amended by striking “October 1, 2023” and inserting “October 1, 2028”.

subsection.heading: Retroactive Effective Date.—
subsection.content: The amendment made by subsection (a) shall take effect as if enacted on October 1, 2023.

section.heading: OTHER AMENDMENTS.
section.chapeau: Section 102 of the Congressional Award Act (2 U.S.C. 802) is amended—

paragraph.content: in subsection (a), by striking “Each medal shall consist of gold-plate over bronze, rhodium over bronze, or bronze and shall be struck in accordance with subsection (f).”; and
in subsection (f)(1), in the second sentence, by striking “Subject to subsection (a), the” and inserting “The”.

signature.role: Speaker of the House of Representatives.
Vice President of the United States and President of the Senate.
